mapping.a_element: knot invariants derived from Wilson loop observables in Chern-Simons theory
mapping.b_element: elements in the Homfly skein theory representing Murphy operators in the Hecke algebra

mapping.a_element: integral invariants capturing the spectrum of M2 branes ending on M5 branes in resolved conifold geometry
mapping.b_element: geometric homomorphism from the Homfly skein of the annulus to the centre of the Hecke algebra

mapping.a_element: all-orders agreement in N between Wilson loop observables and topological string quantities
mapping.b_element: generating function techniques describing images of skein elements in terms of Murphy operators

brief_justification: While both papers involve knot invariants and algebraic structures (Chern-Simons theory/Hecke algebra), their core mechanisms differ: Paper A relies on large N dualities and topological string theory on Calabi-Yau geometries, whereas Paper B uses skein theory and combinatorial representations of Murphy operators in the Hecke algebra. The mapping elements are superficial and do not reflect shared underlying machinery.
